answer: They were telling me a story a while ago.
reasoning: Menele marks ‘they’; na-o-me-lee = tell-to-me + remote (‘me’) + past 3sg/3rd (‘lee’) → ‘were telling … a while ago’.

answer: He is telling them a story.
reasoning: Nalime gives subject ‘he’; na-te-la-ne = tell-to-them + near/present (‘la’) + present (‘ne’) → ‘is telling them’.

answer: I was going a while ago.

reasoning: Nibie = breadfruit; -fe = ‘for’; wa-la-be = go + near future + 3sg → ‘He will be going … soon’.
wa-fo-me-lee = go-to-him + remote (‘me’) + 3sg past (‘lee’) → ‘He was going to him a while ago’.
Nibie = breadfruit; blulu = boil; ne-me-be = (boil) + remote future + 3sg → ‘will be boiling in a while’.

answer: Kwomtari verbs are templatic: VERB-PREFIX (+ object pronoun) + TIME DISTANCE + SUBJECT/AGR. Roots: na- ‘tell (a story)’, ne- ‘boil’, wa- ‘go’; blulu is the lexical verb ‘boil’ used with subjects like water/breadfruit. Object pronouns: o ‘me’, te ‘them’, fo ‘him(‑self)’. Time distance: la = present/near (now/soon), me = remote (a while ago/in a while). Subject/agreement: fe = 1sg future, ie = 1sg past, lee = 3sg (present with la, past with me), be = 3sg future, ne = 3pl present. Nouns precede the verb; -fe on a noun marks ‘for/to’ (goal).
reasoning: Summary of observed morphemes and how translations were derived from them.
